# Talk Page Section Extractor

## What
Extract threaded discussion structure from talk page wikitext: sections, replies, dates, and participants. Feed into the analyze pipeline to enrich talk page correlation (L1-02).

## Why
L1-01 fetches talk page revisions. L1-02 will match them to article revisions by timestamp. But talk pages have internal structure — each `== Section ==` is a discussion thread, each `:`-prefixed line is a reply, each `*` bullet is a vote or comment. Extracting this structure before L1-02 correlation gives the correlator richer deterministic facts. Instead of "talk page was edited within 3 days of this article edit," the signal becomes "a new RFC thread opened on the talk page 2 days before this content change, with 4 participants discussing sourcing."

On fandom wikis, talk page structure reveals dispute resolution patterns: an RFC thread opened, 8 editors joined the discussion, consensus was declared with a timestamp and permalink.

## Context
Read first:
- `packages/analyzers/src/section-differ.ts` — existing section extraction (works on article pages, uses `== heading ==` regex)
- `packages/analyzers/src/wikitext-parser.ts` — `extractHeadingMap`, `extractAnchorSnippet` utilities
- `packages/ingestion/src/mediawiki-client.ts` — `fetchTalkRevisions()` (from L1-01)
- `packages/evidence-graph/src/schemas/evidence.ts` — `EventType` union, `EvidenceEvent` shape
- `packages/analyzers/src/index.ts` — barrel exports

## Implementation
1. Add `"talk_thread_opened" | "talk_thread_archived" | "talk_reply_added"` to the `EventType` union in evidence.ts
2. Create `packages/analyzers/src/talk-section-parser.ts` with:
   ```ts
   interface TalkThread {
     heading: string;
     startedAt?: string; // extracted from signature timestamps
     replies: TalkReply[];
     participants: string[]; // extracted from signatures
     isResolved: boolean; // marked with {{resolved}} or {{done}} template
   }
   interface TalkReply {
     depth: number; // count of `:` prefix
     text: string;
     author?: string;
     timestamp?: string;
   }
   ```
3. Export `parseTalkThreads(wikitext: string): TalkThread[]` — extracts threaded structure
4. Export `diffTalkThreads(before: TalkThread[], after: TalkThread[]): TalkThreadChange[]` — detects new/closed threads, new replies
5. Export `buildTalkThreadEvents(beforeWikitext: string, afterWikitext: string, fromRevId: number, toRevId: number, timestamp: string): EvidenceEvent[]`
6. Integrate into `runAnalyze` in `packages/cli/src/commands/analyze.ts`: when page is a talk page (starts with `Talk:`), run talk thread parsing alongside existing section differ
7. Export from `packages/analyzers/src/index.ts`

## Invariants
- L1 only — deterministic parsing, no NLP
- Thread headings use the same `== heading ==` pattern as the section differ — reuse existing extraction where possible
- Signature extraction is heuristic: look for `~~~~` or `[[User:...]]` patterns. Incomplete matching is acceptable — unmatched threads are still surfaced with empty participant lists
- Does not interpret sentiment or tone — only extracts structural features (thread count, reply count, participant count, timestamps)
- Talk threads are deterministicFacts, not modelInterpretation

## Acceptance
- [ ] Gate: `bun run build && bun run lint && bun run typecheck && bun run test`
- [ ] Write tests in `packages/analyzers/src/__tests__/talk-section-parser.test.ts`:
  - Single thread with heading and timestamp
  - Multiple threads with replies at different indent depths
  - Thread with `{{resolved}}` template → isResolved = true
  - Page with no threads → empty array
  - Diff: new thread appears → `talk_thread_opened`
  - Diff: thread removed → `talk_thread_archived`
  - Diff: reply added to existing thread → `talk_reply_added`
- [ ] Integration: `fetchTalkRevisions("Wikipedia:About")` and parse threads → non-empty result
- [ ] Exported from `packages/analyzers/src/index.ts`
- [ ] Update ROADMAP.md status for L1-08 to `done`
